_source: decode_script.door_commits() (2026-06-27 p2; routine-DIRECTORY fix 2026-07-10). DOOR = op 0x30 AOT_COMMIT; dest = block+0x0c literal push. dest word value IS the destination st_id: dest_id = f'{w:03X}' (stage=high byte, room=low byte), the identity mapping st_id = '%X%02X' % (stage,room) confirmed by the live CreateFileA trace (DOOR-ROUTING-RE.md: 0x102->ST102, hardcoded 0x101->ST101). The prior stage=low formula ('ST101->ST201') was a byte-order bug, now RETRACTED. 2026-07-10: the slot-5 routine directory is SELF-BOUNDING (see decode_script.subprograms); the old 20-entry cap + phantom-entry heuristic hid ST103's two doors (->ST104/ST105) and the tail routines of ST003/205/402/503/607 (84 rooms with doors, 181 doors).
_editable: Redirect a door = overwrite the 2 LE bytes at dest_push_off in the decompressed blob (the dest st_id, e.g. 0x0102 for ST102) + recompress. ROOM-FILE editable (literal doors only). dest routing itself is EXE-internal (DOOR-ROUTING-RE).
_gating: K78 (2026-07-10): SAT-0 handler 0x48b500 warps iff lock_index==0 OR flag(8, unlock_flag_id) is SET; else it shows the lock message [0x731ab8 + lock_index*4]. Both fields are op-0x30 push literals (rec+0x21=block+0x00, rec+0x20=block+0x04). Doors omit the fields when unlocked (lock_index 0). Per-room key_use lists the op-0x3a SAT-10 records (builder 0x48a020 / consumer 0x48b790): interacting sets flag(8, flag8_id) = satisfies the matching locked door; subtype is the equip-class gate (scene_mgr+0x10b5 {2,8}->1, {1,7}->2). Door flags with no key_use record are script-set (op-0x1c SetFlag(8,K) unlock routines -- the flag-dataflow graph, ITEMS-KEYITEMS-RE-PLAN Phase 3).
_confidence: high = literal (mode 0) dest that maps to a real room id; low = variable/expression dest (mode!=0). The ONLY low-conf door corpus-wide is ST003 routine[4] @+0xfd6: dest = stack[15] (mode 2), assembled at runtime and gated by op-0x07 -- a story-conditional COMPUTED warp with NO single editable dest byte. Its dest SET was symbolically resolved to {ST005, ST000, ST004} (see conditional_dests on the door + AOT-SYSTEM-RE.md); a graph consumer may treat it as a 3-edge conditional node. VM operand model: scd-vm-opcodes.json _operand_model.
_flags: flags = the block+0x44 push literal; builder 0x489750 stores rec+0 = flags|9. built_state (derived, K38 rec+0 & 0xc): 'usable' (0x8) or 'blocked' (0xc -- built blocked-at-rest; corpus: 3 doors, all one-way story transitions). flags bit 0x80 (warp_notify): SAT-0 handler 0x48b524 writes scene_mgr+0x926=1, making the next room-entry step 0x451240 skip its 0x48f420 call (corpus: 2 doors, ST408 dual commits -> ST409). door_type = the block+0x08 word (rec+0x1e door-type/anim id; live-confirmed 0x0107 on ST101's ST102 door).
